verdict: No security-relevant change. The patch adds a new WIL feature gate (Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage) to three functions. In SmbCeCompleteSrvCallConstructionPhase2 the gate selects at runtime between the pre-existing CAS-based VctReferenceEndpointSafe (feature on) and the original unconditional-lock-xadd VctReferenceEndpoint (feature off, retained). VctReferenceEndpointSafe is not new; it exists and is used in the unpatched build. This is staged feature rollout plus telemetry, not a delivered security fix, and no exploitable primitive is demonstrable from the two builds.

changed_functions.name: SmbCeCompleteSrvCallConstructionPhase2
key_changes: Patched function is wrapped in a new WIL feature gate Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (call at 0x1C0011E60, and again at 0x1C0012038 / 0x1C0012107 / 0x1C0012152).

Feature ENABLED path (0x1C0011E71..0x1C0011ED3): SmbCeAcquireSpinLock, SmbCeGetFirstVcEndpoint, then VctReferenceEndpointSafe at call site 0x1C0011EAA with 'test al,al' return check; on failure sets STATUS_CONNECTION_DISCONNECTED (0xC000020C) and releases the lock without using the endpoint.

Feature DISABLED path (0x1C0012044..0x1C0012090) is RETAINED and runs the original logic: SmbCeGetFirstVcEndpoint then the unconditional-lock-xadd VctReferenceEndpoint at call site 0x1C001207E, return value not used.

VctReferenceEndpointSafe is not introduced by the patch: it exists in the unpatched build at 0x1C0016354 (patched 0x1C00168E4) with 9 callers unpatched / 10 patched. The unconditional VctReferenceEndpoint (unpatched 0x1C000F710, patched 0x1C000EE90) still has 6 callers in both builds; none were removed.

Patched build also switches the gated blocks to SmbCeAcquireSpinLock/SmbCeReleaseSpinLock wrappers, duplicates the binding-teardown block, and adds a feature-gated telemetry call SmbCeErrorInd at 0x1C00122E2.
changed_functions.security_notes: Not a delivered security fix. The original unconditional-increment path (VctReferenceEndpoint, lock xadd at 0x1C000F722 in the helper) is retained and reachable when Feature_84197691 is disabled; the safe CAS helper it optionally routes through already exists in the unpatched build. The default enablement of the feature is not encoded in the driver image. Whether the unpatched unconditional path is a genuinely reachable use-after-free is not established by these builds and is not asserted; the endpoint is fetched and referenced under the connection spinlock at [connection+0x784]. No controllable free, pool reclaim, or read/write/control-flow primitive is demonstrable. Prior claims of a specific pool tag, async-teardown race window, KASLR/SMEP/HVCI/CFG interaction, or privilege escalation are unsupported and removed.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED VctReferenceEndpoint (0x1C000F710) - unconditional increment:
0x1C000F71D: mov ebx, 1
0x1C000F722: lock xadd [rcx+8], ebx    ; unconditional refcount increment, no zero-check
0x1C000F72B: lea r8, "Ref %d->%d"
0x1C000F76B: mov eax, ebx              ; returns new refcount

VctReferenceEndpointSafe (present in BOTH builds; unpatched 0x1C0016354, patched 0x1C00168E4):
0x1C00168F3: mov ebx, [rcx+8]          ; read refcount
0x1C00168F9: test ebx, ebx
0x1C00168FB: jz  0x1C0016964           ; refcount==0 -> return false (al=0)
0x1C00168FD: lea edx, [rbx+1]
0x1C0016900: mov eax, ebx
0x1C0016902: lock cmpxchg [rcx+8], edx ; conditional atomic increment
0x1C0016907: jnz retry
0x1C0016951: mov al, 1                 ; return true
0x1C0016964: xor al, al                ; return false

UNPATCHED SmbCeCompleteSrvCallConstructionPhase2 (0x1C0006070) - single path:
0x1C0006126: call ExAcquireSpinLockExclusive  ; [connection+0x784]
0x1C0006141: call SmbCeGetFirstVcEndpoint
0x1C0006155: call VctReferenceEndpoint         ; unconditional, return not used
0x1C000615A: and qword [r14+0x148], 0

PATCHED SmbCeCompleteSrvCallConstructionPhase2 (0x1C0011E10) - feature-gated, both paths present:
0x1C0011E60: call Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C0011E6C: jz  0x1C0011EE2                    ; feature OFF -> skip safe block
; feature ON:
0x1C0011E7C: call SmbCeGetFirstVcEndpoint
0x1C0011EAA: call VctReferenceEndpointSafe
0x1C0011EB5: test al, al
0x1C0011EB7: jnz  proceed                       ; failure -> 0xC000020C, release, drop endpoint
; feature OFF (retained original):
0x1C0012038: call Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1C001203F: jnz  0x1C0012099                   ; feature ON -> skip old block
0x1C0012050: call SmbCeGetFirstVcEndpoint
0x1C001207E: call VctReferenceEndpoint          ; unconditional path retained
0x1C0012083: and [rbx+0x148], r12

changed_functions.name: GetDialectInfoFromNegotiateResponse
key_changes: Adds Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage-gated telemetry writes on the error/unsupported-dialect branches (records a status code plus a source-line constant into a local structure) and reorders some ETW/trace emission.
Dialect-detection and size-validation logic is unchanged.
WPP trace GUID reference reflects the new build.

changed_functions.name: SmbCeUpdateTransportDispatchVectors
key_changes: Inside the spinlock the patched version now sets byte [connection+0x782]=1 at 0x1C0035A54 before the conditional store and clears it to 0 afterward; the unpatched version only cleared it to 0. The +0x782 byte is an in-progress marker on the connection object.
Registers holding the connection object and the argument were reshuffled (rsi/rdi/rbx), changing the final store from [rdi+0x150] to [rsi+0x150].

summary: The mrxsmb.sys patch introduces a new WIL feature gate, Feature_84197691__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage, into three functions. In SmbCeCompleteSrvCallConstructionPhase2 (server-call construction completion) the gate selects at runtime between the pre-existing CAS-based reference helper VctReferenceEndpointSafe (feature on, with a checked return that yields STATUS_CONNECTION_DISCONNECTED on failure) and the original unconditional-lock-xadd VctReferenceEndpoint (feature off, fully retained). VctReferenceEndpointSafe is NOT new: it exists in the unpatched build at 0x1C0016354 and is already used at nine call sites; the unconditional VctReferenceEndpoint still has 6 callers in both builds. The other two changed functions add feature-gated telemetry (GetDialectInfoFromNegotiateResponse) and a synchronization in-progress flag write (SmbCeUpdateTransportDispatchVectors). This is staged feature rollout plus telemetry, not a delivered security fix. No use-after-free primitive, controllable free, pool-spray reclaim, or privilege-escalation chain is demonstrable from the two builds; such claims are unsupported and are not made. An independent content-based diff confirms only these three functions changed in logic; all other differences are relocation artifacts.
